description: Durable merge-driven framework task queue. Keep tasks ordered for deterministic next-task preparation.

tasks.title: Starter kit / bootstrap pack
tasks.why_now: Provide a minimum viable adoption path before deeper framework completion work.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add a practical starter-kit bootstrap layer that identifies essential, recommended, and optional framework artifacts for adopting this framework in another repository.

tasks.title: Template harmonization pass
tasks.why_now: Align issue/PR/handoff/review packets to one contract before adding more automation.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Run a bounded harmonization pass across issue templates, PR template, handoff packet template, and review packet templates so required fields and terminology are consistent with the continuity charter.

tasks.title: Reporting summary templates
tasks.why_now: Standardize weekly/monthly/quarterly writebacks after templates are harmonized.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add concise reporting summary templates that operationalize weekly hygiene and quarterly adoption/portability writebacks, aligned with existing metrics and cadence guidance.

tasks.title: Automation bundles by profile
tasks.why_now: Translate profile guidance into reusable automation enablement bundles.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Create bounded automation bundle guidance by framework profile (lighter/heavier settings), defining what to enable first and what to defer while preserving invariants.

tasks.title: Adoption examples expansion
tasks.why_now: Lower onboarding cost with concrete adoption traces once templates are aligned.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add a small set of high-signal worked examples that demonstrate starter-kit onboarding and profile-based adoption in bounded issue→PR flows.

tasks.title: Repo transplant checklist
tasks.why_now: Turn portability guidance into an execution-safe migration control list.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Create a durable repo-transplant checklist that converts portability guidance into a step-by-step migration control list with explicit invariants and validation gates.

tasks.title: Operator onboarding pack
tasks.why_now: Provide a first-week path after adoption/transplant flows are concrete.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add an operator onboarding pack that provides a bounded first-week path for new maintainers/agents, including required reading order, first tasks, and validation expectations.

tasks.title: Framework change governance / deprecation policy
tasks.why_now: Define safe retirement and change management as framework surface area grows.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Define framework change governance and deprecation policy for docs/templates/scripts/workflows, including required notice, replacement path, and writeback expectations.

tasks.title: Framework release/versioning guidance
tasks.why_now: Enable predictable release snapshots after governance semantics are defined.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add framework release/versioning guidance for this repository, including version increment rules, release note expectations, and consumer upgrade communication paths.

tasks.title: Setup intent schema and application model
tasks.why_now: Define the durable setup contract before building the executable apply-setup engine.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add a durable setup intent schema and application model that defines required/optional setup inputs, profile/default mapping rules, expected artifact mutations, and ready-to-work success criteria.

tasks.title: Setup profiles and intent examples
tasks.why_now: Provide concrete reusable setup profiles and example intents before the apply-setup engine is built.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add a bounded setup profile catalog with concrete reusable profiles and machine-readable setup-intent examples that make the setup contract immediately understandable and testable.

tasks.title: Apply-setup and readiness flow
tasks.why_now: Move from documented setup modeling to an executable apply-setup and readiness validation flow.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add a first executable apply-setup flow (scripts/apply-setup.sh), a readiness validator (scripts/check-setup-readiness.sh), and a bootstrap runbook (docs/runbooks/apply-setup.md) that connects setup intent to apply flow to readiness result.

tasks.title: Brain factory foundation
tasks.why_now: Generalize the hub into an executable brain factory: architecture, manifest contract, core command catalog, registry, onboarding spec, and the cross-platform adapter seam.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Establish the brain-factory foundation under brain-factory/: architecture doc + ADR, brain.manifest schema, core-commands catalog, registry (version/releases/learnings-inbox/propagation), onboarding engine spec (inspect-first), and the adapters seam.

tasks.title: Brain factory core command set
tasks.why_now: Author the core commands (stable base names, prefixed per brain via command_prefix) in both Claude Code skill and Copilot prompt forms so every brain inherits a working, portable operating layer.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Author each core command in brain-factory/brain-template/03-templates/agent-commands/core/ as a SKILL.md plus a matching .prompt.md, baking the open/close ritual and the declared model/effort tier per the core-commands catalog.

tasks.title: Inspect-first onboarding engine
tasks.why_now: Build the executable inspector and applier so the hub can provision a new brain or adopt an existing repo without clobbering working artifacts.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Implement the onboarding engine under brain-factory/adapters/ (python source + bash/powershell wrappers): a read-only inspector that emits a gap report, and an applier that instantiates the brain template or augments an existing repo per operator decisions, writing brain.manifest.json.

tasks.title: Capabilities generator, docs-mesh, and CI intent gate
tasks.why_now: Make the brain self-extending and drift-proof: generate the capabilities map from code, validate the docs mesh, and gate features that ship without a capability entry.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Implement the capabilities generator, docs-mesh checks (links/diagrams/freshness), and the CI intent gate as cross-platform tasks, plus the brain-side workflow templates that run them.

tasks.title: Manifest and catalog CI validation
tasks.why_now: Protect the core/extension contract: validate brain.manifest.json against its schema and keep the core-commands catalog in parity with shipped commands.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Add a hub CI check (scripts/check-brain-manifest.sh) that validates brain.manifest.example.json against the schema and verifies the core-commands catalog matches the shipped core command directories.

tasks.title: Onboard Acme (first managed brain)
tasks.why_now: Validate the factory end-to-end by onboarding the renamed Legacy (Acme) as the first brain, retiring its Drive-as-source-of-truth model and fixing known drift.
tasks.suggested_prompt: Run inspect-first onboarding on the Acme project: create acme-autonomy-system from the brain template, migrate the Drive-only context into GitHub, retire Drive-as-truth, resolve the Postgres 16/17 drift, and rename Legacy to Acme across the repo as work proceeds.

tasks.title: Retrofit Northwind into a managed brain
tasks.why_now: Bring the donor system under hub management once the loop is proven, so it also receives framework improvements (donor now, managed brain later).
tasks.suggested_prompt: Retrofit northwind-autonomy-system to the brain template: add brain.manifest.json, map its existing assets to core modules vs extensions inspect-first, and enable xs-upgrade/xs-learn against the hub registry.
